title: The Macro Minute: Who will step up to prevent a global debt refinancing air pocket?

Skipper here Darius Dell to present our
macro minute for Friday March 14 2025 as
always we'll start with the executive
summary from today's lead off morning
note if you would like to review the
analysis accoring these conclusions and
to see if you need to make any changes
in your portfolio to remain on the right
side of Market risk you need to be a
client of 42 macro so today's key macro
question is who will step up and prevent
Global debt refinancing air pocket our
answers are China's February Credit Data
affords us an opportunity to shine a
bright light of global liquidity
Dynamics in a year that requires a
significant acceleration in global
liquidity to prevent a destabilizing
collapse in Risk asset valuations this
is due to the significant acceleration
in global debt refinancing demand the
fastest in a decade if Global liquidity
which is Shand for Global investor
balance sheet capacity does not expand
materially in the coming months there
will likely be a global debt refinancing
air pocket AS Global Capital allocators
demand lower valuations higher yields
wider credit spreads and more favorable
terms to roll over existing debt
obligations in fact it could be argued
that the global debt refinancing air
pocket we've been warning about for
several quarters has commenced already
if so that means the risk appetite of
global capital allocators is already
being curtailed by near unprecedented
policy uncertainty and president Trump's
adopting of our strategy to kid and sync
the US economy it may be the case that a
weak dollar corelates with global
Capital destruction initially before
ultimately providing a Tailwind to
Global liquidity by giving foreign
central banks confidence to ease
aggressively and supporting the growth
rate of global household savings
corporate profits and trade it may also
be the case that the space between phase
one and phase two of this process is the
global debt refinancing air pocket that
we have been warning about with the
global private sector likely pulling
back and made slowing growth declining
confidence and plunging asset values and
global trade likely about to contract
sharply due to the trade War President
Trump started we need a central bank to
step up and materially expand its
balance sheet soon unfortunately we have
yet to see evidence of this bullish
Dynamic president Trump will force the
fed's hand regarding QE eventually that
said hoping for j po to supply the Trump
Administration with liquidity on a
proactive basis is countered to the
reactive nature with which the FED is
historically supplied liquidity
